ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Andrew Schultz, via ZOOM, counsel for Andrea Leung, via ZOOM, Administrator.

The court advises counsel regarding paying close attention to the allocation of disbursements and the court's expectation of the Administrator's compliance regarding the preceding in future accountings of this trust. The court also queries counsel regarding the request to sell the Antioch property without a court order. Mr. Schultz advises that currently, the property is being rented and the current tenants have shown interest in buying the home. Additionally, Mr. Schultz advises that if the house was placed on the market, any potential buyers may be discouraged due to the home being occupied.

After discussion, the Court takes the Accounting Petition in this matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-12-295304 Conservatorship of Sheldon A. Dulberg and
PTR-14-298220 In Re: Sheldon A. Dulberg 2010 Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Andrea Leung (Trustee).
Garry Cohen for and with Arthur Dulberg (Objector).
Sheldon Dulberg (Conservatee) also present.

Mr. Cohen stated that based on his Client's concerns the issue remaining is the basis for the Trustee's fees, that they feel it is more appropriate to use an hourly rate going forward and raised the issue of the trips to Oakland for haircuts stating that it is costly to the estate. Mr. Schultz stated that they have always flagged the trips to Oakland for the Court as it is an unusual expense, that it has been approved in the accountings, that when he saw the schedules he raised the issue of these expenses and suggested that they stop, and that these are services that Sheldon wanted. Mr. Schultz also stated that the reason for the Saturday outings was to give the Brothers time away from each other, that Arthur cares for his Brother at night, that the Brothers argue and yell at each other, that he believes the trips were appropriate and that the trips stopped before Arthur's objections. The Third Account and Report, etc., and the Amended Second Account and Report, etc. are under submission.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, for and with petitioner Andrea Leung; conservatee Sheldon A. Dulberg.

The Court inquired regarding the financial necessity for the rental of the Antioch property, and the possible alternative of keeping the property secure. Mr. Siracusa responded that the property could be boarded up, but that sometimes this can be an invitation to housebreakers, and would also be distressing to the property's neighbors. He stated that Mr. Dulberg has sufficient assets so that there is not an immediate need for rental income, but that the trustee has been required to invade principal for payment of property taxes, and that there will be further need to reach into principal if Mr. Dulberg's care needs increase. Ms. Leung stated that if the property were rented, she would estimate the rental income at about $2500 per month. She stated that Mr. Dulberg has not lived in the property for at least six years, and that he currently visits the property only once a month for about 10 minutes. She added that if he were to live there, he would require caretaking services on a 24/7 basis. Mr. Siracusa noted that it was the intention merely to rent the property and not to sell it, so that if Mr. Dulberg's health improved he could return to live in the premises at the conclusion of the lease term. The Court asked Mr. Dulberg if he wished to say anything. Mr Dulberg responded that he wanted to live there (at the Antioch property). The matter taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)
